title: Language Modeling as a Foundation
concept: Language modeling is the foundation task behind modern text generation and pretrained representation models. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Text Generation.'

exercises.prompt: If a language model reads 'The weather is warm and...', which word is it most likely to predict next most likely to predict?
exercises.explain: Language models learn by guessing the next word. In this case, 'sunny' is the most likely word to complete the sentence naturally.

cards.title: The Secret Behind AI
cards.body: How does an AI learn to write stories, translate languages, and help you code? It all starts with a simple game of 'guess the next word'!

cards.title: What is Language Modeling?
cards.body: Language modeling is the task of training a computer to predict the next word in a sequence based on the words that came before it. By mastering this simple task, AI models learn grammar, common sense, and facts about the world.

cards.title: The Smart Keyboard
cards.body: Think of a language model like a smart keyboard on your phone. When you start typing 'Happy...', it guesses that the next word is probably 'Birthday!' because it has seen that pattern thousands of times before.

cards.title: Predicting the Next Word
cards.body: If you give a language model the prompt: 'The sky is...', it calculates the probabilities of all possible next words. It might assign a 90% chance to 'blue', a 9% chance to 'cloudy', and a 0.0001% chance to 'banana'.

cards.title: Myth: AI Thinks Like a Human
cards.body: People often think AI language models actually understand the meaning of life and have human feelings. In reality, they are just super-advanced statistical calculators estimating which word should logically come next based on their training.

cards.title: Guessing vs. Copying
cards.body: Instead of memorizing sentences, a language model builds a map of probabilities to guess what comes next.

left.text: Estimates the most likely next word based on context.

right.text: Just copies and pastes exact sentences from a database.

exercises.prompt: What is the fundamental task that language models are trained on?

options.text: Predicting the next word in a sentence
Translating English directly into math formulas
Counting how many letters are in a document

exercises.explain: By learning to predict the next word on billions of pages of text, the model naturally absorbs grammar rules, facts, and writing styles.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Language modeling is the foundation that allows AI to generate creative text and stories.
exercises.explain: Language modeling is the foundation task that teaches models patterns, which they then use to generate long paragraphs, essays, and stories.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the core definition of a language model.
exercises.sentence: A language model's main job is to predict the next word.
exercises.explain: Language models are trained to look at a sequence of words and predict the most likely word to follow.

exercises.prompt: Which sentence was completed by a properly trained language model predicting the next words?

options.text: It was a dark and stormy night in the city.
It was a dark and banana elephant yellow.
It was a dark and running quickly jumped.

exercises.explain: The first statement is generated by a language model completing the sentence logically. The other two are random words thrown together without predicting the correct sequence.

exercises.prompt: Why is language modeling used as the foundation task for training large AI models?

options.text: To write computer programs from scratch
To learn the patterns, grammar, and facts of human language
To count the total number of words in a dictionary
To translate text into binary code

exercises.explain: Language modeling (predicting the next word) serves as the core pretraining task that teaches models grammar, facts, and reasoning before they are tuned for specific text generation applications. Next up: Text Generation.
